calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-09-2018 Set For Trial On Aug-05-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Montana Baker, Esq. of Hersh & Hersh, 415.441.5544, appearing for Plaintiff Oliver Davis;
Corbyn Porter, Esq. of Wood Smith Hank Berman, 925.222.3419, appearing for Defendant Curtis N. Crane, MD.

Matter called. The Court and counsel confer regarding the disputed medical reports. Argument/discussions are held. At the conclusion of argument/discussions the Court takes a brief recess for counsel to meet and confer.

At the second call of the matter, the Court and counsel confer regarding the proposed spreadsheet by Defense counsel and the narrowed subset of operative reports as suggested by the Court. Additional argument is held.

Ruling: ARGUED. PLAINTIFF OLIVER DAVIS' Notice of Motion and Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions - At the conclusion of argument and meet and confer by counsel, the Court suggested that counsel meet and confer regarding the issue(s) presented this morning. The Court noted that 60 days would be appropriate.

Counsel to prepare a form or order and submit it to the Court for review and signature. (see Official Court Reporter's transcript this date for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brendan Cannon, Esq. 415-441-5544 of Hersh and Hersh for Plaintiff Oliver Davis.
Corban Porter, Esq. 925-222-3419 of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendant CURTIS CRANE, M.D.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CURTIS CRANE, M.D.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendant Curtis Crane's demurrer to the third and fourth cause of actions for intentional and negligent misrepresentation in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Oliver Davis is overruled as to both causes of action. Mr. Davis adequately alleged that (1) Dr. Crane made three distinct preoperative statements which were false; (2) Dr. Crane knew the statements were false or acted with reckless disregard as to their truth; (3) he made them to induce Mr. Davis to consent to the surgery; (4) Mr. Davis justifiably relied on Dr. Crane's misrepresentations; and (5) as a result, Mr. Davis was injured. Stone v. Foster (1980) 106 Cal.App.3d 334, 344 is distinguishable because the doctor in that case performed an intended surgery that produced the results he promised. In contrast, Mr. Davis alleges that Dr. Crane misrepresented information to induce Mr. Davis into consenting to a surgery that did not produce the promised results. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Corban Porter, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, Tel. 925.222.3419, for defendant and moving party Curtis Crane, M.D.;
Nancy Hersh, Esq. of Hersh & Hersh, Tel. 415.444.5544, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court MODIFIED its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CURTIS CRANE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows: Defendant Crane's demurrers to the first and second causes of action are overruled. Even if the court accepts Dr. Crane's premise that failure to obtain informed consent is a species of medical negligence, plaintiff's theories of recovery (negligent surgery and failure to obtain informed consent) are not duplicative. There is no practical purpose in requiring plaintiff to replead his failure to obtain informed consent claim under the rubric of medical negligence in the first cause of action. Dr. Crane is not prejudiced by any purported pleading defect. The demurrers to the intentional and negligent misrepresentation claims are SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR # 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org.
